step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("association between immune dysregulation and preeclampsia")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First write at most two sentences analyzing the current evidence. Then state your final answer choice.
The retrieved evidence consistently links disrupted immune regulation at the maternal-fetal interface to preeclampsia through multiple mechanisms: elevated pro-inflammatory cytokines (e.g., TNF-α, IL-6), reduced anti-inflammatory cytokines (e.g., IL-10), increased CD4+ T-cell infiltration, excessive M1 macrophages, and deficient T-regulatory cell function—all of which impair trophoblast invasion and spiral artery remodeling, critical steps in establishing proper placental perfusion. Furthermore, lower expression of protective molecules like HLA-G and diminished Treg activity directly correlate with compromised immune tolerance and higher incidence of preeclampsia.
\boxed{\text{A}}
